one_line_ask: Grade four raw deli photos to one cohesive gritty-warm look, cut the hero sandwich onto transparency, source a butcher-paper backdrop, vectorize the Deli & Dough wordmark, and bind all the prepped parts into the client's authored 2880x2304 Illustrator banner template to export a print-ready Uber Eats hero banner.
brief: Deli & Dough is a scrappy New-York-style sandwich shop in Western Sydney — gritty, bold, a little chaotic, think NY deli meets Nonna's kitchen meets street culture, not polished or corporate. We're setting up our Uber Eats storefront and need our hero banner finished. We already have the assets; they just need to be pulled together so they look like they were shot in one image — one cohesive, intentional photo, NOT a collage. Here is the hand-off: (1) an authored Adobe Illustrator banner template (banner_template.ai) built to EXACTLY 2880px wide x 2304px high (the Uber Eats hero spec), containing named placement frames — a full-bleed background frame, a hero-sandwich frame, three supporting food frames, and a wordmark frame — plus a small data-merge slug row so the tagline/handle text binds in; (2) four raw phone-shot deli photos — the hero pastrami-on-rye stacked high, a supporting Italian sub, a supporting basket of house pickles, and a supporting drip-coffee cup; (3) our wordmark as a flat cream-on-red PNG; (4) direction to back everything on a gritty butcher-paper / deli-counter texture.
